summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that an unauthenticated attacker can upload a malicious SVG file as a product review attachment via the POST /api/product/{id}/review endpoint. The SVG file is stored on the public disk without sanitization and the embedded JavaScript payload remains intact, enabling stored cross-site scripting when any user navigates to the file URL.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction pipeline should cause the malicious SVG file (containing a <script> tag with the EVOHUNT_XSS_PROOF marker) to be stored unsanitized on the public disk, accessible via a direct web URL.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh builds the Docker image, installs Bagisto, creates a test product, then uploads a malicious SVG file via the unauthenticated review API endpoint. The SVG file is found on disk at storage/app/public/review/{id}/{hash}.svg with the EVOHUNT_XSS_PROOF JavaScript marker intact, confirming the file was stored without SVG sanitization.
verification_method: The verify.sh script searches the public storage directory for the uploaded SVG file, reads its content, and checks for the presence of the EVOHUNT_XSS_PROOF marker string. It also attempts to fetch the file via the web server URL to confirm the malicious content is served to clients.
evidence_produced_by_run: The POST /api/product/{id}/review request with the SVG attachment returned HTTP 200 (review accepted).
The SVG file was found on disk at storage/app/public/review/{id}/{hash}.svg.
The file content contains the unsanitized <script> tag with the EVOHUNT_XSS_PROOF marker.
The ProductReviewAttachmentRepository did not apply the Sanitizer trait, unlike SearchRepository which does sanitize SVG uploads.
The file is served via the public/storage/ symlink, bypassing Laravel's SecureHeaders middleware.
limitations_or_blockers: The PoC verifies file storage and content integrity. Full browser-based JavaScript execution would require a headless browser to navigate to the SVG URL.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable ref, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
